subject: Statistics
title: The T-Test
description: A short lesson to help you apply The T-Test.

hook.text: A new teaching method is tried on 25 students. Their scores are higher. Is it real or random noise?

buildup.text: The t-test handles small samples where the population standard deviation is unknown.

discovery.text: It uses the sample's own standard deviation and adds extra uncertainty through wider tails.

twist.text: William Gosset invented it at Guinness Brewery. His employer banned publishing, so he used the name 'Student.'

climax.text: The t-test is the workhorse of science. Most published results comparing two groups rely on it.

punchline.text: Small sample? Unknown variance? Use the t-test.

quiz.question: What makes the t-test different from the z-test?
options: It uses the sample's own standard deviation and accounts for extra uncertainty in small samples
It only works with large samples
It doesn't require any assumptions about the data
